prerequisites: A reachable RSS feed URL. RSS is a dialect of XML, so the feed must be a valid RSS document served over HTTP(S) at a stable, publicly retrievable address.
Network access from where pm runs to the host serving the feed.

authMethods.name: None (public feed URL)
authMethods.summary: The connector exposes no secret fields and the RSS 2.0 specification defines no authentication mechanism for retrieving a feed. You supply only the feed URL, which must be publicly retrievable without credentials.

setupSteps.title: Locate the feed URL
setupSteps.body: Find the RSS feed address you want to ingest. This is the URL of an XML document conforming to the RSS specification (a top-level rss element with a version attribute). Confirm it loads in a browser or via curl and returns RSS XML.

setupSteps.title: Configure the url field
setupSteps.body: Set the required url config field to the feed address. This is the only configuration the connector takes; there are no secret fields to provide.

setupSteps.title: Validate the feed
setupSteps.body: Ensure the URL returns a well-formed RSS document. Per the spec, all RSS files must conform to the XML 1.0 specification, so an invalid or non-XML response will not parse.

sources.title: RSS 2.0 specification - W3C Feed Validation Service
